summary:
The heights were measured from two separate but similar-sized samples; thus, an unpaired or independent-samples t-test should be used due to its suitability for smaller, independently drawn random samples where population standard deviations might not be known accurately enough without additional assumptions being made about distributional properties fitting perfectly into either parametric conditions required by Z-testing requirements (>N=30 per condition). Chi-Square testing isn't suitable as it pertains more towards categoric variable relationships instead of numerical comparisons across distinct populations directly comparable through mean differences analysis via T-statistics framework given herein described contextually specific parameters met well under traditional inferential statistics guidelines outlined above regarding applicability considerations amongst these common hypothesis testing methods available today. Agent1 concludes using an Independent Samples t-test because both datasets meet typical prerequisites including relatively equal size (<30 individuals per dataset allowing robustness despite potential minor deviation away fully satisfying strict Central Limit theorem demands often seen when larger n values exist).

The correct choice based upon this information aligning closely matches option 'Independent Sample' which corresponds logically leading us toward finalizing answer at \( \boxed{A}\).
